This generosity is a hallmark of the island's cultural **heritage**, a living reminder of the values that bind society together. It is a time when social barriers are **cast** aside, and the commonality of **humanity** is celebrated. While the event is ostensibly **religious**, its significance extends far beyond the confines of any single creed. It is a cultural phenomenon that the government has rightly chosen to **designate** as a national treasure. In a world that often emphasizes division, this event stands as a powerful symbol of unity, reminding us that we are all fellow travelers on the same road.

employ.definition: to pay someone to do work or a job
employ.example: With his restaurant business growing fast, Mr. Finch decided to employ more people to serve the customers.
employ.example_tw: 隨著餐廳生意快速成長，芬奇先生決定僱用更多人手來服務顧客。

corporation.definition: a very large company or business
corporation.example: Most global corporations operate in many countries and prefer employees who can speak more than one language.
corporation.example_tw: 大多數的跨國公司在許多國家營運，並且偏好會說一種以上語言的員工。

suffer.definition: to experience pain or trouble because of something
suffer.example: Uncle Harry suffers from a heart condition, so the doctors told him to cut down on fatty foods.
suffer.example_tw: 哈利叔叔患有心臟病，所以醫生告訴他要減少攝取油膩的食物。

terminal.definition: (of a disease or condition) that can't be cured and will lead to death
terminal.example: When Grandma learned she had terminal cancer, she decided to make the most of the time left.
terminal.example_tw: 當奶奶得知她患有末期癌症時，她決定要好好把握剩下的時間。

illness.example: These days, many people suffer from illnesses such as diabetes or cancer caused by unhealthy lifestyles.
illness.example_tw: 現今，許多人因不健康的生活方式而罹患如糖尿病或癌症等疾病。

tremendously.example: Aunt Mimi was tremendously thankful when the firefighters rescued her cat from the tree.
tremendously.example_tw: 當消防員將咪咪阿姨的貓從樹上救下來時，她非常感謝。

laughter.definition: the act or sound of laughing
laughter.example: Every time Dennis tells that funny story, we all burst into laughter.
laughter.example_tw: 每次丹尼斯講那個好笑的故事，我們都會爆笑出聲。

frighten.definition: to scare someone, usually very suddenly
frighten.example: When the kids came around the corner dressed in zombie costumes for Halloween, they frightened the old lady almost to death.
frighten.example_tw: 當孩子們為了萬聖節穿著殭屍服裝從轉角出現時，他們差點把那位老太太嚇死。

contact.definition: the state of people or things touching each other
contact.example: When your hand comes into contact with a surface that's very hot, your body reacts even more quickly than your brain does!
contact.example_tw: 當你的手接觸到非常燙的表面時，你的身體反應甚至比你的大腦還快！

germ.definition: a very tiny living thing that can cause illness or disease
germ.example: Proper cleaning of your contact lenses will kill the germs which may cause a serious eye infection.
germ.example_tw: 適當地清潔你的隱形眼鏡將會殺死可能導致嚴重眼睛感染的細菌。

warmth.definition: kindness, passion, or enthusiasm shown by someone
warmth.example: Agnes felt bad about staying an extra night, but the warmth in Mel's smile assured her that she was still welcome.
warmth.example_tw: 愛麗絲對於多待一晚感到不好意思，但梅爾微笑中的熱情讓她確信她仍然是受歡迎的。

frail.example: After falling ill and spending a month in the hospital, the old man was very frail and could hardly stand up.
frail.example_tw: 在生病並住院一個月後，那位老人變得非常虛弱，幾乎站不起來。

prompt.definition: to cause something to occur or someone to do something
prompt.example: Was it the strange noises or the flashes of light that prompted you to get up and take a look around?
prompt.example_tw: 是奇怪的噪音還是閃光促使你起身四處查看的？

confine.translation: 使離不開(床、輪椅等)；侷限
confine.definition: to force someone sick or injured to stay in bed or in a wheelchair, etc.
confine.example: Despite being confined to a wheelchair after the terrible accident, Lara remained as outgoing as ever.
confine.example_tw: 儘管在可怕的事故後只能待在輪椅上，勞拉依然像以往一樣外向。

sparkle.definition: lively brightness, usually showing happiness or confidence; a series of bright flashes made by something shiny
sparkle.example: Although he had been confined to the cave for so long, there was still a sparkle in the explorer's eyes when he was rescued.
sparkle.example_tw: 儘管被困在洞穴裡這麼久，當那位探險家獲救時，他的眼中依然閃耀著神采。

hug.definition: the act of holding someone or something to show warmth or love
hug.example: On her return from abroad, Christina gave her sister a nice warm hug.
hug.example_tw: 從國外回來後，克莉絲汀給了她妹妹一個溫暖的擁抱。

drowsy.example: Nicole didn't sleep well last night. No wonder she feels so drowsy today.
drowsy.example_tw: 妮可昨晚沒睡好。難怪她今天這麼昏昏欲睡。

stretch.definition: to reach out with an arm or leg to get to something
stretch.example: The book about corporate finance was on the top shelf, so Chuck had to stretch his arm high up to reach it.
stretch.example_tw: 那本關於企業金融的書在書架最上層，所以查克必須把手臂伸得很高才拿得到。

refuse.definition: to answer no to a request or invitation
refuse.example: Nurse Wells offered to take the patient for a walk in the garden, but he refused because he felt drowsy.
refuse.example_tw: 威爾斯護士提議帶病人到花園散步，但他因為感到昏昏欲睡而拒絕了。

hesitate.definition: to pause before doing something because one feels unsure, afraid, or nervous
hesitate.example: When her boyfriend proposed, Kim didn't hesitate about the decision at all. She answered "Yes!" right away.
hesitate.example_tw: 當她男友求婚時，金對這個決定一點也沒有猶豫。她立刻回答了「我願意！」。

innovative.definition: able to come up with new and creative ideas for using or doing something
innovative.example: Successful inventions often involve people taking an innovative approach to solving a problem.
innovative.example_tw: 成功的發明通常涉及人們用創新的方法來解決問題。

promise.definition: to make something likely to happen or to show signs of something
promise.example: The dark clouds promised rain, so we decided to bring umbrellas before heading out for a walk.
promise.example_tw: 烏雲預示著要下雨了，所以我們決定出門散步前帶上雨傘。

material.definition: things used to take part in a certain activity
material.example: Our art teacher first explained how to make the lanterns, and then handed out some paper, wire, and other materials.
material.example_tw: 我們的美術老師首先解釋了如何製作燈籠，然後分發了一些紙、鐵絲和其他材料。

mention.definition: to briefly talk or write about something without going into detail
mention.example: Jacob mentioned the trip plan to his parents, and they liked the idea.
mention.example_tw: 雅各向父母提到了旅行計畫，他們很喜歡這個主意。

structure.definition: something that is built by putting together different materials
structure.example: Before the workers completed the new building, tests were conducted to see how well the structure could withstand strong winds.
structure.example_tw: 在工人完成新大樓之前，進行了測試以檢視該結構體能多好地承受強風。

capable.example: After taking lessons for a year, Martha was capable of playing several songs on the guitar.
capable.example_tw: 上了一年的課後，瑪莎已經能夠用吉他彈奏幾首歌曲了。

collaborate.definition: to work with someone else or others for a special purpose
collaborate.example: Throughout the years, Louis Vuitton has collaborated with various artists for new collections.
collaborate.example_tw: 多年來，路易威登與多位藝術家合作推出新系列。

architect.definition: someone who designs buildings for a living
architect.example: The architect promised to design a beautiful building for the corporation's new head office.
architect.example_tw: 這位建築師承諾要為公司的新總部設計一棟美麗的建築。

graduate.definition: someone with a college or university degree
graduate.example: Debbie's new employer is also a graduate of Oxford, so the two shared many fond memories from their college days.
graduate.example_tw: 黛比的新雇主也是牛津大學的畢業生，所以兩人分享了許多大學時期的美好回憶。

construct.definition: to build something that is usually large and requires a lot of labor and materials
construct.example: Paris's famous Eiffel Tower was constructed in just over two years using around 18,000 pieces of iron.
construct.example_tw: 巴黎著名的艾菲爾鐵塔僅用兩年多一點的時間，使用了約18,000塊鐵件建造而成。

slightly.example: It took Janice slightly longer to find a job than the other graduates, but in the end, it was worth the wait.
slightly.example_tw: 珍妮絲找工作比其他畢業生花了稍微長一點的時間，但最終這等待是值得的。

rank.definition: to compare people or things by placing them in order according to performance, quality, ability, etc.
rank.example: On a list of the world's best universities, schools such as MIT, Stanford, and Cambridge often rank in the top 1%.
rank.example_tw: 在全球最佳大學名單上，麻省理工學院、史丹佛大學和劍bridge大學等學校經常排在前1%。

unique.example: The coconut crab is a unique type of crab that is capable of climbing trees and breaking coconuts open with its claws!
unique.example_tw: 椰子蟹是一種獨特的螃蟹，牠能夠爬樹並用牠的爪子打開椰子！

adjust.definition: to make slight changes to suit the conditions or requirements better
adjust.example: This software allows you to adjust the color, brightness, and size of your photos.
adjust.example_tw: 這個軟體可以讓你調整照片的顏色、亮度和尺寸。

rely.definition: to use, need, or depend on someone or something
rely.example: The small mountain kingdom of Bhutan relies heavily on tourism to keep its domestic economy going.
rely.example_tw: 不丹這個小山國嚴重依賴旅遊業來維持其國內經濟。

feedback.definition: opinions about something, usually with advice or suggestions on how to make it better
feedback.example: After receiving a lot of constructive feedback from customers, the company was able to improve their product a great deal.
feedback.example_tw: 在收到大量來自顧客的建設性回饋後，公司得以大幅改善他們的產品。

element.definition: a basic part or condition that is necessary
element.example: Maintaining the right temperature at all times is a key element in food transportation.
element.example_tw: 隨時保持正確的溫度是食品運輸中的一個關鍵要素。

stability.definition: the condition of not changing much over time
stability.example: Water rushed into the hole at the front of the ship and soon the stability of the whole ship was in danger.
stability.example_tw: 水湧入船頭的洞口，很快整艘船的穩定性就岌岌可危了。

route.definition: a specific road or way taken to reach or get to somewhere
route.example: Since the main road to the beach was under construction, the family had to take a different route.
route.example_tw: 由於通往海灘的主要道路正在施工，這家人不得不走不同的路線。

explore.definition: to try out or investigate new things, usually in order to find or learn something
explore.example: Reports suggest that there might be alien life on Mars, so scientists will need to further explore the planet.
explore.example_tw: 報告顯示火星上可能有外星生命，所以科學家需要進一步探索這顆行星。

humankind.definition: the human race; all people as a whole
humankind.example: With all the environmental damage it is causing, humankind is destroying planet Earth.
humankind.example_tw: 隨著所造成的環境破壞，人類正在摧毀地球。

universe.definition: stars, planets, moons, and everything else that exists in space
universe.example: Compared with other stars in the universe, our sun is actually quite small.
universe.example_tw: 與宇宙中的其他恆星相比，我們的太陽其實相當小。

fur.example: Polar bears are covered in thick fur that protects them from the bitter cold.
fur.example_tw: 北極熊覆蓋著厚厚的毛皮以保護牠們免受嚴寒。

enable.definition: to make someone able to do something
enable.example: Powerful telescopes enable us to observe distant planets, moons, and stars in this enormous universe.
enable.example_tw: 強大的望遠鏡使我們能夠觀察到這個巨大宇宙中遙遠的行星、月亮和恆星。

upright.example: It's better for your back if you sit upright in your chair and don't bend over your desk.
upright.example_tw: 如果你挺直坐在椅子上，而不是彎腰趴在桌上，對你的背部比較好。

generous.definition: taking pleasure in giving freely; not stingy or selfish
generous.example: It was very generous of Jen to give her niece all that expensive jewelry.
generous.example_tw: 珍非常慷慨地把所有昂貴的珠寶都給了她的姪女。

conflict.example: Both nations believed the island belonged to them, so they soon came into conflict with each other.
conflict.example_tw: 兩個國家都認為該島嶼屬於他們，所以他們很快就發生了衝突。

distribution.definition: how something is spread or shared among a group or over an area
distribution.example: The large forest fire has affected the distribution of wild animals within the national park.
distribution.example_tw: 大規模的森林大火影響了國家公園內野生動物的分布。

benefit.definition: a good, useful, or helpful effect; an advantage or gain
benefit.example: Everyone agreed that free education for all would solve a lot of problems and be of great benefit to society.
benefit.example_tw: 所有人都同意，全民免費教育將解決許多問題，並對社會大有裨益。
benefit.note:
用法小提示：

及物 (vt.): 「事情」 benefit 「人」。
The new library will benefit the students.

不及物 (vi.): 「人」 benefit from 「事情」。
The students will benefit from the new library.

slaughter.definition: to kill an animal for its meat
slaughter.example: Pigs are often transported to Taipei before being slaughtered so that the meat will be fresh.
slaughter.example_tw: 豬隻通常在被宰殺前被運到台北，以確保肉質新鮮。

underneath.definition: below or under (a cover, mask, lid, etc.)
underneath.example: Every night, the little boy asks his mother to check if any monsters are hiding underneath his bed.
underneath.example_tw: 每天晚上，小男孩都會叫媽媽檢查床底下是否躲著怪物。

freeze.definition: to suffer terrible, often deadly, cold
freeze.example: If you go out in the snow without a coat and gloves, you'll freeze to death.
freeze.example_tw: 如果你不穿外套、不戴手套就到雪地裡去，你會凍死的。

victim.definition: a person who suffers or dies because of an accident, crime, attack, or disease
victim.example: At the moment, victims of the huge flood are taking shelter in the local school hall.
victim.example_tw: 目前，大洪水的受災者正在當地的學校大廳避難。

miserable.definition: very sad or unhappy due to mental or physical suffering
miserable.example: Danny felt miserable when he was unable to find the watch that his grandfather had given to him.
miserable.example_tw: 當丹尼找不到祖父給他的手錶時，他感到非常難過。

hollow.definition: empty on the inside; filled with air only
hollow.example: Water pipes are usually completely hollow inside, so the water can flow through them freely.
hollow.example_tw: 水管內部通常是完全中空的，這樣水才能自由地流過。

furious.example: People were furious at the government for not distributing funds evenly among schools in the area.
furious.example_tw: 民眾對於政府未能在該地區學校間公平分配資金感到非常憤怒。

theft.definition: the action of stealing something from someone
theft.example: The criminal didn't only rob the bank. He also committed a car theft while trying to get away from the police.
theft.example_tw: 那名罪犯不僅搶劫了銀行，還在試圖逃離警方時偷了一輛車。

punish.definition: to force someone to suffer because he or she has done something wrong
punish.example: Benjamin was punished by his parents for coming home late again.
punish.example_tw: 班傑明因為再次晚歸而受到父母的懲罰。

banish.definition: to force someone to leave a place or country and never come back
banish.example: The prince was banished from the kingdom for trying to kill the king.
banish.example_tw: 王子因試圖殺害國王而被逐出王國。

torture.definition: to force someone to suffer a lot of pain, often on purpose and without any mercy
torture.example: Professional athletes torture themselves with long workouts and practice routines in order to become the best.
torture.example_tw: 職業運動員為了成為最頂尖的選手，用長時間的訓練和練習來折磨自己。

civilization.definition: advanced, well-developed, and well-organized human society
civilization.example: The new port and bridges brought civilization to the remote island.
civilization.example_tw: 新的港口和橋樑為這座偏遠的島嶼帶來了文明。

joyous.definition: making one feel very happy; filling one with happiness
joyous.example: Hannah celebrated her daughter's birth with friends and family; it was a joyous event.
joyous.example_tw: 漢娜與親友一同慶祝女兒的誕生；那是一個令人愉快的活動。

accompany.example: Whenever Allen goes for a walk in the park, his furry black dog Butch always accompanies him.
accompany.example_tw: 每當艾倫去公園散步時，他毛茸茸的黑狗布奇總是陪伴著他。

essential.definition: related to the basic nature or the most important part
essential.example: The essential difference between these two religions is that one has only one god while the other has many.
essential.example_tw: 這兩種宗教本質上的差異在於一個只有一位神，而另一個則有多位神。

concept.definition: an idea one can imagine or think up
concept.example: Very young children don't have the concept of time, so they don't understand how long a minute or an hour is.
concept.example_tw: 非常年幼的孩童沒有時間的概念，所以他們不了解一分鐘或一小時是多久。

mourn.definition: to feel very sad about one's death and/or act in a way that shows this sadness
mourn.example: According to Confucius, every child should mourn the loss of a parent for at least three years.
mourn.example_tw: 根據孔子的說法，每個孩子都應該為父母的逝去哀悼至少三年。

sorrow.example: Though Angela felt sorrow at parting from her high school friends, she looked forward to studying abroad.
sorrow.example_tw: 雖然安琪拉對於要和高中朋友分離感到悲傷，但她很期待出國留學。

occasion.definition: a special or significant event or happening
occasion.example: On New Year's Eve, there were lots of fireworks at Sydney Harbour Bridge, and around a million people turned up for the occasion.
occasion.example_tw: 在跨年夜，雪梨港灣大橋施放了許多煙火，大約有一百萬人為此場合而來。

reunion.definition: a special occasion for a family, class, etc. to get together after being apart for a while
reunion.example: Every five years, all my cousins living abroad return to Taiwan for a big family reunion. It's always great to see everybody again.
reunion.example_tw: 每隔五年，我所有住在國外的表兄弟姊妹都會回到台灣來場大型的家庭團聚。能再次見到大家總是件很棒的事。

anticipate.example: Months before the birth of their child, the couple started to anticipate holding the little baby in their arms.
anticipate.example_tw: 在孩子出生前的幾個月，這對夫妻就開始期盼能將小寶寶擁入懷中。

arrival.definition: the act of one's coming to or reaching a certain place
arrival.example: Upon the passengers' arrival at the airport, customs officials searched all their bags for drugs and dangerous weapons.
arrival.example_tw: 旅客一抵達機場，海關人員便搜查他們所有的行李，尋找毒品和危險武器。

decorate.definition: to make something look more attractive by putting things on it
decorate.example: In the week before the performance, the students decorated the stage with balloons and colorful lights.
decorate.example_tw: 在表演前一週，學生們用氣球和彩燈裝飾舞台。

preference.definition: a stronger liking of something compared to another or others
preference.example: Whenever Justin goes to a café, he has a strong preference for cold drinks.
preference.example_tw: 每當賈斯汀去咖啡館時，他都偏愛冷飲。

reception.definition: a way in which someone or something is received, welcomed, or accepted
reception.example: Minutes after the president's arrival in North Korea for the first time, he was treated to a very warm and friendly reception.
reception.example_tw: 在總統首次抵達北韓的幾分鐘後，他受到了非常熱烈友好的歡迎。

feast.definition: to eat and drink a lot with great pleasure, often to celebrate a special occasion
feast.example: For Thanksgiving dinner, the reunited relatives feasted on roast turkey, mashed potatoes, and pumpkin pie.
feast.example_tw: 感恩節晚餐時，團聚的親戚們盡情享用了烤火雞、馬鈴薯泥和南瓜派。

dawn.definition: the beginning of the day, when sunlight first appears
dawn.example: Roosters are known to crow at dawn.
dawn.example_tw: 公雞以在黎明時分啼叫而聞名。

transform.definition: to change something or someone in nature, shape, or form
transform.example: The old ship has been transformed into a seafood restaurant where customers can feast on fresh fish and crab every day.
transform.example_tw: 這艘舊船已被改造成一間海鮮餐廳，顧客每天都可以在那裡大啖新鮮的魚和螃蟹。

resemble.definition: to look or seem like something or someone else
resemble.example: Although the brothers do resemble each other in terms of looks, their personalities couldn't be more different.
resemble.example_tw: 雖然這對兄弟在外表上很相像，但他們的個性卻截然不同。

participate.definition: to take part or be involved in something such as an activity or a sporting event
participate.example: The school held a charity garage sale and invited all the students and their parents to actively participate in the event.
participate.example_tw: 學校舉辦了一場慈善舊貨出售會，並邀請所有學生和他們的家長踴躍參加。

mock.definition: to make fun of or treat someone or something with very little respect by copying
mock.example: It's not polite to mock people's accent when they speak a foreign language.
mock.example_tw: 嘲笑別人說外語的口音是不禮貌的。

recognize.definition: to be aware of and accept as true or real
recognize.example: Though it is recognized that the new medication has several negative side effects, no better treatment currently exists.
recognize.example_tw: 雖然大家都承認這種新藥有幾種負面副作用，但目前沒有更好的治療方法。

inevitable.definition: impossible to avoid; sure to happen
inevitable.example: It is inevitable that more natural disasters will occur in the future if we don't do something now to slow down climate change.
inevitable.example_tw: 如果我們現在不採取行動減緩氣候變遷，未來發生更多自然災害是不可避免的。

cycle.definition: a continuous process in which the steps are always repeated in the same order
cycle.example: In its natural cycle, fruit falls to the ground, leaving behind seeds that grow into new trees.
cycle.example_tw: 在其自然循環中，果實掉落到地上，留下種子長成新的樹木。

annual.definition: happening or done once per year
annual.example: The Frankfurt Book Fair is an annual event that takes place every October.
annual.example_tw: 法蘭克福書展是每年十月舉行的年度盛事。

await.example: The advertisement reads, "At the Dolphin Bay Hotel, a warm reception and amazing ocean views await our honored guests."
await.example_tw: 廣告上寫著：「在海豚灣飯店，熱情的接待和絕佳的海景正等待著我們尊貴的客人。」

escape.definition: to get away from or avoid
escape.example: Greg pretended to have a headache in order to escape doing the dishes, but his mother didn't fall for it.
escape.example_tw: 葛瑞格假裝頭痛以逃避洗碗，但他媽媽沒有上當。

blossom.definition: a flower or group of flowers, especially those found on blooming fruit trees
blossom.example: Apple blossoms are beautiful white flowers, but did you know they're actually poisonous to cats?
blossom.example_tw: 蘋果花是美麗的白色花朵，但你知道牠們對貓有毒嗎？

rustle.definition: to make a soft, crackling sound, like the noise of leaves or paper moving
rustle.example: While walking through the park at dusk, I heard something rustle in the bushes, which made me stop and look around nervously.
rustle.example_tw: 傍晚在公園散步時，我聽到灌木叢中有些沙沙聲，這讓我停下腳步緊張地四處張望。

landscape.definition: everything you can see when you look over a large area of land, especially in the countryside
landscape.example: The desert landscape seemed harsh but stunningly beautiful at the same time.
landscape.example_tw: 沙漠的景色看似嚴酷，卻同時又美得驚人。

splendor.definition: very fine, grand, or impressive beauty
splendor.example: As the climbers reached the mountain peak, the splendor of the sunrise over the valley took their breath away.
splendor.example_tw: 當登山者到達山頂時，山谷日出的壯麗景色讓他們屏息。

previously.example: Alison works as a receptionist now, but previously she was a flight attendant.
previously.example_tw: 艾莉森現在是一名接待員，但她先前曾是空服員。

capital.definition: a country's main or the most important city
capital.example: No one in the group had previously visited London, so they decided to start their UK trip in the capital.
capital.example_tw: 團體中沒有人之前去過倫敦，所以他們決定從首都開始他們的英國之旅。

boast.definition: to have something of great value to be proud of
boast.example: Our new community sports center boasts six indoor basketball courts, an Olympic-size swimming pool, and four tennis courts.
boast.example_tw: 我們新的社區運動中心擁有六個室內籃球場、一個奧運規格的游泳池和四個網球場。

historic.translation: 歷史悠久的；歷史上著名的
historic.example: On April 12, 1961, Yuri Gagarin became the first person to fly into space. It was a historic day for humankind.
historic.example_tw: 1961年4月12日，尤里·加加林成為第一位飛入太空的人。那對人類來說是歷史性的一天。
historic.note:
用法比較 (Usage Note):

historic: 形容在歷史上非常重要、有名、或具重大意義的人事物。帶有「創造歷史」的感覺。

historical: 泛指一切與歷史有關的、關於過去的人事物。範圍較廣。

entrance.definition: a gate or a door where one can enter a room, building, or place
entrance.example: A large fish which represents the baseball team is found at the entrance to the stadium.
entrance.example_tw: 在體育場的入口處，有一條代表棒球隊的大魚。

magnificent.definition: very beautiful and impressive; wonderful to see
magnificent.example: Yangmingshan's magnificent cherry blossoms only last for about a month, so make sure you get there in time!
magnificent.example_tw: 陽明山壯麗的櫻花只持續大約一個月，所以務必及時趕到！

breathtaking.definition: so beautiful or impressive that one almost forgets to breathe
breathtaking.example: All travelers who have a chance to witness the Northern Lights agree that they are of breathtaking beauty.
breathtaking.example_tw: 所有有機會目睹極光的旅客都同意，它們的美麗令人屏息。

leisurely.example: On his day off, Carlos spent a leisurely afternoon playing catch on the lawn with his kids.
leisurely.example_tw: 卡洛斯休假時，悠閒地在草坪上和孩子們玩接球遊戲度過了一個下午。

stunning.translation: 撼動人心的；令人驚訝的
stunning.example: Marcia really looked forward to the fashion show and couldn't wait to see the models in their stunning new clothes.
stunning.example_tw: 瑪西亞非常期待這場時裝秀，迫不及待地想看到模特兒穿上令人驚豔的新衣服。

delicate.example: The exhibit includes a number of delicate Chinese paintings from the sixteenth century.
delicate.example_tw: 展覽中包括了數幅十六世紀的精美中國畫。

distinctive.definition: easy to recognize, identify, or notice
distinctive.example: Due to their distinctive sun-like shape and purple coloring, passion flowers in blossom are easy to recognize.
distinctive.example_tw: 由於西番蓮花獨特的太陽狀外形和紫色，盛開時很容易辨認。

elegant.definition: showing a simple and appealing beauty in design or appearance
elegant.example: The designer's latest collection features elegant evening wear that blends classic and modern styles.
elegant.example_tw: 這位設計師的最新系列以融合古典與現代風格的雅致晚宴服為特色。

appearance.example: The actress's red hair and brown eyes give her a very distinctive appearance.
appearance.example_tw: 這位女演員的紅髮和棕眼給了她非常獨特的外表。

literature.definition: written works such as novels, plays, and poems
literature.example: Novels such as Dream of the Red Chamber and A Tale of Two Cities are very famous works of literature.
literature.example_tw: 像《紅樓夢》和《雙城記》這樣的小說是世界文學中非常著名的作品。

deliberate.example: Zoo employees didn't want to frighten the rhino, so they approached the animal with very slow, deliberate movements.
deliberate.example_tw: 動物園員工不想嚇到犀牛，所以他們以非常緩慢、小心翼翼的動作靠近牠。

destination.example: Bali, Indonesia, has become one of the most popular holiday destinations for travel bloggers and Instagram influencers.
destination.example_tw: 印尼的峇里島已成為旅遊部落客和Instagram網紅最受歡迎的度假目的地之一。

slope.definition: a surface or piece of land that is higher at one end than the other
slope.example: The children enjoyed sliding down the grassy slope in the park.
slope.example_tw: 孩子們喜歡在公園的草坡上向下滑行。

tumble.definition: to fall down quickly and suddenly, especially with a rolling movement
tumble.example: The books tumbled off the shelf during the earthquake and were scattered across the floor.
tumble.example_tw: 地震時，書本從書架上滾落，散落一地。

bruised.definition: showing or having blue, black, or purple skin due to an injury
bruised.example: The day after her scooter accident, Rachel's right arm was bruised and sore.
bruised.example_tw: 騎車發生意外的隔天，瑞秋的右臂又瘀青又痛。

superior.definition: better or greater than someone or something else
superior.example: Many people believe that fresh fruit is superior to canned fruit. They say it tastes better and is healthier.
superior.example_tw: 很多人認為新鮮水果優於罐頭水果。他們說新鮮水果味道更好，也更健康。

intimately.definition: in a very detailed or close way
intimately.example: After living there for twenty years, Mrs. White knew the neighborhood intimately, from its quiet streets to its busy markets.
intimately.example_tw: 在那裡住了二十年後，懷特太太對這個社區了如指掌，從安靜的街道到繁忙的市場都一清二楚。

skillfully.example: The dancer made each move skillfully, and her performance was amazing.
skillfully.example_tw: 舞者熟練地做出每一個動作，她的表演令人驚嘆。

crop.definition: a plant grown in large amounts, especially as food
crop.example: Rice is the main crop grown in many parts of Asia, especially in regions with warm climates.
crop.example_tw: 稻米是亞洲許多地區種植的主要作物，尤其是在氣候溫暖的地區。

path.definition: a way or track that is made for walking
path.example: We followed the narrow path through the woods. It led us to a small clearing.
path.example_tw: 我們沿著穿過樹林的小徑走，它帶我們到了一片小空地。

navigate.translation: (人)行走、確認方向;(船隻、車輛或飛機等)航行
navigate.definition: (of people, ships, cars, or planes, etc.) to find and follow a route or direction in order to reach a destination
navigate.example: The hikers used a map to navigate through the dense forest and reach the campsite.
navigate.example_tw: 登山客們用地圖在茂密的森林中指引方向，以到達營地。

outsider.definition: a person who does not belong to a society, group, organization, etc.
outsider.example: The small town rarely welcomed outsiders, so tourists often felt unwelcome.
outsider.example_tw: 這個小鎮很少歡迎外來者，所以遊客常常感到不受歡迎。

adapt.definition: to change oneself in order to deal more successfully with a new situation
adapt.example: After moving to a new country, Jerry had to adapt quickly to the different culture and language.
adapt.example_tw: 搬到一個新的國家後，傑瑞必須迅速適應不同的文化和語言。

cling.definition: to hold on tightly to someone or something
cling.example: The boy clung tightly to his mother's hand as they crossed the busy street.
cling.example_tw: 過馬路時，小男孩緊緊抓住媽媽的手。

twinkle.definition: to shine with an unsteady light
twinkle.example: The lights on the Christmas tree twinkled, spreading a warm and cheerful glow throughout the room.
twinkle.example_tw: 聖誕樹上的燈光閃爍著，在整個房間裡散發出溫暖而愉悅的光芒。

pity.definition: to feel sorry for someone because of his or her situation
pity.example: The stray dog looked thin and weak, so the boy pitied it and offered it some food.
pity.example_tw: 那隻流浪狗看起來又瘦又弱，所以小男孩同情牠，並給了牠一些食物。

awe.definition: feelings of respect and slight fear
awe.example: The tourists looked up at the pyramids with awe, amazed by their great size and ancient history.
awe.example_tw: 遊客們敬畏地仰望著金字塔，對其巨大的規模和古老的歷史感到驚訝。

smoothness.definition: the quality a surface has when it is completely flat and even, without any rough areas or holes
smoothness.example: Patty admired the smoothness of the silk scarf. It was so soft and pleasant to touch.
smoothness.example_tw: 佩蒂欣賞著絲巾的平滑，它摸起來是如此柔軟舒適。

opposition.definition: the act of strongly disagreeing with somebody or something
opposition.example: There was little opposition to the plan during the meeting. Most people thought it was a good idea.
opposition.example_tw: 會議期間對該計畫幾乎沒有反對意見，大多數人都認為這是個好主意。

peculiar.example: The town is known for its peculiar traditions, such as hosting an annual wife-carrying championship.
peculiar.example_tw: 這個小鎮以其奇特的傳統而聞名，例如舉辦一年一度的背老婆錦標賽。

confront.definition: (of a problem or difficulty) to appear and need to be dealt with by somebody
confront.example: When their boss suddenly died, the workers were confronted with a major problem. Who would run the company now?
confront.example_tw: 當他們的老闆突然去世時，工人們面臨一個大問題：現在由誰來經營公司呢？

dilemma.definition: a situation in which one has to make a difficult choice between two things
dilemma.example: Chris faced a dilemma about whether to stay at his current job or accept a new offer.
dilemma.example_tw: 克里斯面臨一個兩難的困境：是要留在目前的工作，還是接受一個新的工作機會。

nevertheless.definition: in spite of what has just been said
nevertheless.example: Ray is hard-working and careful; nevertheless, he sometimes makes mistakes.
nevertheless.example_tw: 雷工作努力且細心；儘管如此，他有時還是會犯錯。

gaze.definition: to look at something or someone for a long time
gaze.example: The old man gazed at the photograph, remembering the happy days of his youth.
gaze.example_tw: 老人凝視著照片，回憶著他年輕時的快樂時光。

envision.definition: to imagine or expect something to happen in a particular way
envision.example: Peter envisions himself running his own business one day. He is already making plans for it.
envision.example_tw: 彼得想像自己有一天能經營自己的事業，他已經在為此制定計畫了。

flee.definition: to leave a person or a place quickly, in order to escape from danger
flee.example: The villagers fled their homes when the floodwaters rose. They ran to higher ground for safety.
flee.example_tw: 當洪水上漲時，村民們逃離家園，他們跑到地勢較高的地方避難。

theory.definition: a recognized collection of ideas explaining the reasons behind something existing or occurring
theory.example: Darwin's theory of evolution is one of the most important sets of ideas in the history of science.
theory.example_tw: 達爾文的演化論是科學史上最重要的思想體系之一。

psychology.definition: the scientific study of the human mind and how it works
psychology.example: Wes Craven majored in psychology, which taught him a lot about the human mind and thus helped him become a successful horror film director.
psychology.example_tw: 韋斯·克雷文主修心理學，這讓他對人類心智有很多了解，進而幫助他成為一位成功的恐怖片導演。

demonstrate.definition: to show or prove that you have a particular feeling or opinion
demonstrate.example: Luke has demonstrated much excitement and interest in his new job.
demonstrate.example_tw: 路克對他的新工作表達了極大的興奮與興趣。

interpret.definition: to understand or be able to explain something
interpret.example: One can often interpret what a person is saying in a different language simply by observing his or her body language.
interpret.example_tw: 人們通常可以僅藉由觀察他或她的肢體語言，來理解一個人在說不同語言時所說的話。

stiff.definition: hard to move or bend, especially regarding muscles, body parts or movements
stiff.example: After a long, hard day in the mines, the miners' arms were stiff and sore from all the digging.
stiff.example_tw: 在礦場辛苦工作了一整天後，礦工們的手臂因為挖掘而感到僵硬和痠痛。

scratch.definition: to rub against with a fingernail or sharp object
scratch.example: According to the article, it's better not to scratch an itchy mosquito bite because that just makes it worse.
scratch.example_tw: 根據這篇文章，最好不要去抓發癢的蚊子叮咬處，因為那只會讓情況更糟。

exhibit.definition: to clearly show or display to others
exhibit.example: The bull exhibited its dislike for the stranger by angrily scratching the ground and pointing its large, sharp horns at her.
exhibit.example_tw: 那頭公牛憤怒地抓地並用巨大尖銳的角指向陌生人，以表現牠對她的不喜歡。

gesture.definition: a movement or action performed with a body part (especially the hands, arms, or head) to communicate something without speaking
gesture.example: The traffic officer made a gesture at the driver to show that she could now safely turn right.
gesture.example_tw: 交通警察向駕駛做出手勢，表示她現在可以安全右轉了。

moreover.example: The Danube is the second longest river in Europe. Moreover, it's the river that flows through the most countries in the world.
moreover.example_tw: 多瑙河是歐洲第二長的河流。此外，它是流經世界上最多國家的河流。

conscious.definition: being aware of something; noticing something
conscious.example: When Jim woke up after the accident, he slowly became conscious of the machine beeping next to his hospital bed.
conscious.example_tw: 當吉姆在意外後醒來時，他慢慢意識到病床旁機器的嗶嗶聲。

observe.definition: to watch or study closely, usually with the aim of recording or learning
observe.example: The class closely observed their yoga teacher's graceful movements so that they could copy her.
observe.example_tw: 學生們仔細觀察瑜珈老師優雅的動作，以便模仿她。

reverse.example: Some say Leila is a liar, but quite the reverse is true. She is one of the most honest people I have ever known.
reverse.example_tw: 有人說蕾拉是騙子，但事實恰恰相反。她是我認識的最誠實的人之一。

significant.example: The researchers couldn't find any significant evidence to support the theory.
significant.example_tw: 研究人員找不到任何顯著的證據來支持這個理論。

circumstance.definition: conditions or factors that together determine or affect a situation
circumstance.example: Under no circumstances can visitors to the museum touch or remove any artworks from the exhibition.
circumstance.example_tw: 在任何情況下，博物館的參觀者都不能觸摸或移動展覽中的任何藝術品。

initially.example: Initially, the bruises on Arthur's leg were deep purple. Then, as they healed, they slowly turned brown.
initially.example_tw: 起初，亞瑟腿上的瘀傷是深紫色的。然後，隨著癒合，它們慢慢變成了褐色。

glance.definition: to take a quick look at someone or something
glance.example: Although Arnold didn't want his date to know he was bored, he still glanced at the clock now and then.
glance.example_tw: 雖然阿諾不想讓他的約會對象知道他很無聊，但他還是不時地瞄一眼時鐘。

automatically.example: Close observation has shown that we automatically raise our eyebrows more when we speak to people we are attracted to.
automatically.example_tw: 仔細的觀察顯示，當我們和我們被吸引的人說話時，我們會不自覺地揚起眉毛。

track.definition: to follow a person or thing, usually with one's eyes or with a special device
track.example: With this app, one can track friends' movements and spot their locations at any time.
track.example_tw: 有了這個應用程式，人們可以隨時追蹤朋友的動向並鎖定他們的位置。

adore.definition: to love or admire someone very much
adore.example: Angie adores the man who saved her cat and believes him to be a hero.
adore.example_tw: 安琪愛慕那位救了她的貓的男人，並認為他是個英雄。

affection.definition: love or liking for someone or something
affection.example: Even after being married for 40 years, the couple still showed a lot of affection to each other.
affection.example_tw: 即使結婚了40年，這對夫妻仍然對彼此表現出很多喜愛。

interact.definition: to communicate, work, or spend time with others
interact.example: Scientists grouped children with different mother tongues together to see how they would interact with one another.
interact.example_tw: 科學家將母語不同的兒童分組在一起，觀察他們如何互動。

meaningful.definition: effective and important; being of great value
meaningful.example: All the employees had prepared a lot for the meeting, so they had a very meaningful discussion and came up with some great ideas.
meaningful.example_tw: 所有的員工都為會議做了充分的準備，所以他們進行了非常有意義的討論，並提出了一些很棒的想法。

fellow.definition: in the same situation or position; belonging to the same group or unit
fellow.example: Don and his fellow workers usually spend their lunch break eating and chatting together.
fellow.example_tw: 唐和他的同事通常在午休時間一起吃飯聊天。

abuse.example: The slave suffered terrible abuse at the hands of his cruel masters and was often beaten, tortured, or left hungry.
abuse.example_tw: 這名奴隸在殘忍的主人手中遭受可怕的虐待，經常被毆打、折磨或挨餓。

highlight.definition: the most exciting or interesting part of something; the best part or high point
highlight.example: The highlight of Benny and Liz's European honeymoon was their romantic picnic under the Eiffel Tower.
highlight.example_tw: 班尼和麗茲的歐洲蜜月之旅中最精彩的部分是他們在艾菲爾鐵塔下的浪漫野餐。

economy.definition: the overall condition of a country with regard to how much it produces, trades, and consumes, and how large its money supply is
economy.example: Several large banks in our country have run out of money, so it will take a long time before the economy is able to recover.
economy.example_tw: 我國幾家大型銀行的資金已經耗盡，因此經濟需要很長一段時間才能復甦。

demand.definition: the need or want for something, such as a particular product or service
demand.example: The small factory is struggling to produce enough soy yogurt products to satisfy the demand of customers.
demand.example_tw: 這家小工廠正努力生產足夠的豆漿優格產品以滿足顧客的需求。

illegally.definition: in a manner not allowed by the law
illegally.example: The fishermen were caught using nets illegally in restricted waters.
illegally.example_tw: 漁民被抓到在限制水域非法使用漁網。

lure.definition: to tempt someone or an animal with a reward in order to lead them somewhere, especially into a trap or a dangerous position
lure.example: In the story, the hunter manages to lure the bear into a deep hole with raw meat and a pot of honey.
lure.example_tw: 在這個故事中，獵人設法用生肉和一罐蜂蜜將熊引誘進一個深洞。

herd.definition: a group of animals (especially herbivores like zebra or deer) that move around and find food together
herd.example: At the dairy farm, we saw a large herd of cattle in the field; there must have been at least three hundred of them.
herd.example_tw: 在乳牛場，我們在田野裡看到了一大群牛；那裡至少有三百頭。

brutal.example: Sadly, many innocent women and children were killed in the army's brutal attack on the little village.
brutal.example_tw: 遺憾的是，許多無辜的婦女和兒童在軍隊對這個小村莊的殘暴攻擊中喪生。

operator.definition: an individual or company that runs a business or provides a professional service
operator.example: Due to increasing demand, one more ferry operator will begin providing transport to the island during the summer.
operator.example_tw: 由於需求增加，另一家渡輪業者將在夏季開始提供前往該島的運輸服務。

mercilessly.definition: in a manner showing no pity or compassion
mercilessly.example: The cruel king mercilessly punished all criminals by ordering his soldiers to torture them.
mercilessly.example_tw: 殘暴的國王冷酷無情地懲罰所有罪犯，命令士兵折磨他們。

strike.definition: to hit someone, something, or an animal with one's hand or a weapon
strike.example: After hearing the news, Roy was so angry that he struck the wall with his fist.
strike.example_tw: 聽到這個消息後，羅伊非常生氣，用拳頭猛擊牆壁。

commit.definition: to do something immoral or illegal
commit.example: Terry committed a criminal act when he broke into his neighbor's house and stole a loaf of bread.
commit.example_tw: 泰瑞闖入鄰居家偷了一條麵包，犯下了罪行。

obedient.definition: willing to follow or obey the rules, orders, or instructions given by someone in a higher position
obedient.example: Helix the guide dog is very obedient to his owner and always does what she asks immediately.
obedient.example_tw: 導盲犬Helix對主人非常服從，總是立即照她的要求去做。

tame.definition: to train a wild animal to be obedient, comfortable with people and easier to control
tame.example: By feeding the wolf daily for a month, Scott was finally able to tame it and become its master.
tame.example_tw: 透過每天餵食這隻狼一個月，史考特終於馴服了牠並成為牠的主人。

vulnerable.definition: in a condition where it is easy to be hurt
vulnerable.example: Without a helmet, cyclists are particularly vulnerable to head injuries in an accident.
vulnerable.example_tw: 如果沒有戴安全帽，騎自行車的人在事故中頭部特別容易受傷。

permanent.definition: lasting forever or for one's whole life; not able to be reversed or corrected
permanent.example: Think twice before you get a permanent tattoo because you'll need to live with it for the rest of your life.
permanent.example_tw: 在刺永久性刺青之前要三思，因為你需要終生與它共存。

adequate.definition: enough or satisfactory in terms of the amount or quality required
adequate.example: One large pizza should be adequate for two people, but it's slightly small for three.
adequate.example_tw: 一個大披薩對兩個人來說應該足夠，但對三個人來說稍微小了一點。

exhaustion.definition: extreme tiredness; total lack of energy
exhaustion.example: As soon as the runner crossed the finish line, she fell to the ground, weak and stiff with exhaustion.
exhaustion.example_tw: 跑者一越過終點線，就因筋疲力盡而虛弱僵硬地倒在地上。

complex.definition: not simple or basic; complicated or difficult to understand or interpret
complex.example: Some psychological disorders are highly complex and thus difficult to treat, even for professional psychologists.
complex.example_tw: 有些心理障礙非常複雜，因此即使對專業心理學家來說也很難治療。

intelligent.definition: smart or exhibiting an advanced ability to think and learn quickly
intelligent.example: Though he was considered highly intelligent, Albert Einstein failed an entrance exam for a college in Zurich when he was sixteen.
intelligent.example_tw: 雖然愛因斯坦被認為非常聰明，但他十六歲時在蘇黎世一所學院的入學考試中不及格。

pilgrimage.definition: a journey to a holy place for religious reasons
pilgrimage.example: There are two main Mazu pilgrimages in Taiwan, and they usually take place in January and April, depending on Mazu's will.
pilgrimage.example_tw: 台灣有兩個主要的媽祖朝聖活動，通常在 一月和四月舉行，具體時間取決於媽祖的旨意。

unify.definition: to bring individuals or groups together to form one unit
unify.example: The country's first president unified the people and is considered the founding father of the nation.
unify.example_tw: 該國的第一任總統凝聚了人民，被認為是國父。

exaggeration.definition: when a speaker describes things as far more, less, greater, smaller, better, worse, etc. than they really are
exaggeration.example: Newspaper reports should be written without exaggeration and personal opinions.
exaggeration.example_tw: 報紙報導應不帶誇張和個人意見地撰寫。

undoubtedly.example: Physical contact, especially in the form of a hug, is undoubtedly one of the best ways to comfort someone feeling miserable.
undoubtedly.example_tw: 身體接觸，尤其是擁抱，無疑是安慰感到痛苦的人的最佳方式之一。

exact.example: Though the travel agent couldn't give us the exact price for an economy class seat, she promised it would be under $30,000.
exact.example_tw: 雖然旅行社無法給我們經濟艙座位的確切價格，但她保證會在三萬元以下。

authorities.definition: the person, group, organization, or body that is in control and makes the rules
authorities.example: Ashley has to contact the transportation authorities, namely the police or the Traffic Department, to get her towed car back.
authorities.example_tw: 艾希莉必須聯繫交通當局，即警察或交通部門，才能取回她被拖吊的車。

cast.definition: to throw, usually with care and for a specific purpose (cast-cast-casting)
cast.example: In one quick movement, the cowboy cast the rope around the pole and pulled it tight.
cast.example_tw: 牛仔一個快速的動作，將繩子拋向柱子並拉緊。

cast_n_actors.translation: (一齣戲劇或一部電影的)全體演員

devotion.definition: religious worship or strong religious feeling
devotion.example: Catherine's lifelong devotion to her faith guided every decision she made, big or small.
devotion.example_tw: 凱瑟琳終生對信仰的虔誠指引了她所做的每一個大大小小的決定。

throng.example: The politician was surrounded by a throng of reporters asking about his recent shocking statements, but he refused to reply to them.
throng.example_tw: 那位政治人物被一大群記者包圍，詢問他最近令人震驚的言論，但他拒絕回應。

explosive.example: The explosive sounds coming from the stadium where a famous pop singer was putting on a concert really angered the nearby residents.
explosive.example_tw: 一位著名流行歌手正在體育場舉辦演唱會，傳來的震耳欲聾聲響讓附近的居民非常憤怒。

pierce.definition: (of light, sound, etc.) to suddenly cut through the darkness or silence
pierce.example: With no power supply during the storm, villagers spent the evening watching flashes of lightning pierce the dark sky above.
pierce.example_tw: 暴風雨期間沒有電力供應，村民們整個晚上都在看著閃電劃破上方的漆黑天空。

emerge.definition: to appear or come out of somewhere
emerge.example: A sharp cry of fear pierced the night air as Nicholas saw two large bears emerge from the dark cave.
emerge.example_tw: 當尼可拉斯看到兩隻大熊從黑暗的洞穴中出現時，一聲恐懼的尖叫劃破了夜空。

blessing.definition: help, generosity, or protection prayed for or received from a god
blessing.example: In their prayers, the devoted nuns always ask for the Lord's blessing so that He may guide and protect them.
blessing.example_tw: 虔誠的修女在祈禱中總是祈求主的祝福，以便祂能指引和保護她們。

kneel.definition: to bend down and place a knee or both knees on the ground, usually to pray, propose, or beg for mercy (knelt-knelt-kneeling)
kneel.example: Megan was stunned when Pat knelt down right then and there in the street and asked her to marry him.
kneel.example_tw: 當帕特就在街上跪下向她求婚時，梅根驚呆了。

pavement.example: Any vehicle which will go up the mountain this week should be equipped with snow chains to prevent sliding on the icy pavement.
pavement.example_tw: 本週任何上山的車輛都應配備雪鏈，以防止在結冰的路面上打滑。

hospitality.definition: generous and welcoming treatment shown by a host or hostess to guests or visitors
hospitality.example: On the way out, the guest thanked the hosts again for offering her such warm hospitality during her stay.
hospitality.example_tw: 客人在離開時，再次感謝主人在她逗留期間給予如此熱情的款待。

enthusiastically.definition: exhibiting a lot of excitement, warmth, passion, eagerness, or interest
enthusiastically.example: Fans cheered enthusiastically when the singer emerged onto the stage.
enthusiastically.example_tw: 當歌手出現在舞台上時，歌迷們熱情地歡呼。

facilities.definition: equipment, furniture, buildings, or services that enable the user to do something
facilities.example: Princeton University boasts a splendid range of sports facilities that include football stadiums, a rowing center, and a nine-hole golf course.
facilities.example_tw: 普林斯頓大學擁有一系列極佳的運動設施，包括足球場、划船中心和一個九洞高爾夫球場。

accommodations.definition: a place to stay, usually for a short time
accommodations.example: The hotel offers small but clean rooms to travelers seeking cheaper accommodations.
accommodations.example_tw: 這家飯店為尋求較便宜住宿的旅客提供小而乾淨的房間。

religious.definition: because of or connected to one's faith
religious.example: It's not that the monks don't enjoy meat or fish dishes. They're vegetarian for religious reasons.
religious.example_tw: 並不是僧侶不喜歡肉類或魚類菜餚。他們是因為宗教原因而吃素。

humanity.definition: kindness, consideration, concern, or mercy shown to fellow humans or creatures
humanity.example: If that country's leaders had any humanity, they wouldn't let so many innocent women and children suffer as a result of the war.
humanity.example_tw: 如果那個國家的領導人有一點人性的話，他們就不會讓這麼多無辜的婦女和兒童因戰爭而受苦。

ritual.definition: religious or traditional process or routine that's usually repeated many times
ritual.example: Before the tribe offers the slaughtered beast to the gods, they perform an ancient ritual to clean and bless the flesh.
ritual.example_tw: 在部落將宰殺的野獸獻給神靈之前，他們會舉行古老的儀式來清潔和祝福肉體。

designate.definition: to formally give a person or place a certain position, title, etc.
designate.example: The country's water supply is now so vulnerable that its government has officially designated the crisis a national emergency.
designate.example_tw: 該國的供水現在非常脆弱，政府已正式將這場危機定為國家緊急狀態。

heritage.translation: (一國家或社會所形成的) 遺產
heritage.definition: the history, traditions, and qualities of a country or society
heritage.example: Folk songs appear to be an important part of this country's national heritage.
heritage.example_tw: 民謠似乎是該國國家遺產的重要組成部分。
